comment: Radio metadata for Road Trip Arcade. Audio clips go in radio/clips/ named {era}_{type}_{n}.mp3 (types: id, intro, outro, ad, rain, city, unlock). Songs go in radio/music/ named {era}_{songSlug}.mp3.

70s.name: The Wax Museum
70s.tagline: All killer, no filler, and the killer is groovy.
70s.voiceDirection: Deep male, slow, warm, near-whisper 70s FM announcer. Long pauses.

80s.name: Power 104 — The Razor
80s.voiceDirection: High-energy male rock DJ, fast, loud, gravelly, monster-truck-ad delivery.

tracks.style: Minneapolis electro-funk with dry drum machines, rubbery synth bass, clipped guitar, and a playful falsetto lead
tracks.bio: A one-man funk laboratory whose arcade cabinet allegedly doubles as a mixing desk.

tracks.style: Heartland rock with ringing guitars, piano, saxophone, and a story-first vocal
tracks.bio: A highway-bar five-piece that built its first tour out of borrowed vans and tip-jar money.

tracks.style: Mid-80s old-school hip-hop with a stripped drum-machine beat, scratches, horn hits, and chant hooks
tracks.bio: Three school friends who treated the gym locker room like a championship arena and never stopped bragging.

tracks.style: High-energy glam metal with crunchy guitars, gated drums, gang vocals, and a fireworks-sized chorus
tracks.bio: Blaze, Dagger, Torque, and Kevin legally changed every name but one. Kevin says that is the joke.

tracks.style: Triumphant arena rock with soaring guitars, thunderous gated drums, and a stadium-sized chorus
tracks.bio: They rehearse exclusively on arena loading docks so every chorus remembers where it belongs.

tracks.style: Atmospheric British Isles anthem rock with chiming delay guitars, wide synth pads, and an urgent tenor
tracks.bio: A rooftop-born quartet whose first drummer kept time against a laundromat window.

tracks.style: Nocturnal heartland rock with cracked-mirror imagery, fogbound guitars, and an end-of-the-line chorus
tracks.bio: A late-night road band whose Suno tape was accidentally labeled Nightfall; the chorus knew its real name.

tracks.style: Bright synth-pop with LinnDrum punch, sparkling arpeggiators, and an expressive female lead
tracks.bio: Discovered at a food court payphone and contractually required to make every basement look glamorous.

tracks.style: Dark 80s pop-funk with flashing synths, hard drums, clipped guitar, and an urgent high-tenor lead
tracks.bio: A precision dance-pop solo star whose choreography is treated like classified information.

tracks.style: Late-80s pop and R&B power ballad with electric piano, glossy pads, gated snare, and a soaring female belt
tracks.bio: A meticulous studio perfectionist who records the quiet verse last, after the walls stop shaking.

tracks.style: Romantic goth-pop and new wave with chorus bass, chiming guitar, soft drum machine, and bright melancholy
tracks.bio: They keep every lyric in damp shoeboxes and insist the water damage improves the memories.

tracks.style: Jangly college rock with picked guitars, melodic bass, dry drums, and an earnest conversational vocal
tracks.bio: A left-of-the-dial quartet broadcasting from a basement they affectionately call the dungeon.

tracks.style: British art-pop and new wave with spacious Linn drums, glassy pads, piano, and restrained anthemics
tracks.bio: Former office clerks who wrote their debut on the backs of forms they were supposed to file.

tracks.style: Early-80s pop-funk with syncopated bass, crisp drums, synths, horn stabs, and playful falsetto
tracks.bio: A precision dance-pop solo star whose choreography is treated like classified information.

tracks.style: Piano-led power ballad that builds through strings and guitar into a massive female-vocal finale
tracks.bio: She writes every chorus at sunrise and refuses to record until an actual storm has passed.

tracks.style: Lean new wave reggae-rock with syncopated drums, fretless-style bass, choppy guitar, and a tense tenor
tracks.bio: Their drummer is rumored to be a drum machine wearing sunglasses. He continues to deny it.

tracks.style: Early-80s dance-pop with crisp claps, bright synth bass, clean funk guitar, and cheeky chant vocals
tracks.bio: A style thief in the best sense: one borrowed jacket, one club entrance, one instant pop career.

90s.name: The Dirt
90s.tagline: Whatever. It's fine. It's The Dirt.
90s.voiceDirection: Young female, flat, deadpan, slight vocal fry, college-radio mumble, sighs.

tracks.style: old-school rap-rock, three MCs trading and interrupting each other, live drums, turntable scratches, fuzz bass
tracks.bio: Three friends from Queens who recorded trading verses in an idling station wagon. None of them has a license.

tracks.style: boy-band dance-pop, four-on-the-floor, key-change finale
tracks.bio: Five guys who met at a mall shoe store. Their choreographer quit over 'The Spin.'

tracks.style: boy-band dance-pop, call-and-response backing vocals
tracks.bio: The follow-up single. Steve, the quiet one, secretly writes everything.

tracks.style: nu metal rap rock, gang vocals, turntable scratches
tracks.bio: Nu-metal born in a storage unit. The cinder-block wall they smash is foam.

tracks.style: funky alt-rock, turntable scratches, half-time bridge
tracks.bio: Cincinnati funk-rock. Their DJ is credited only as 'Wristwatch.'

tracks.style: thrash metal, 180 BPM gallop, gang vocals, Viking everything
tracks.bio: Thrash-metal Vikings from Ohio. The singer's real name is Gary.

tracks.style: British post-grunge with brooding verses, thick low guitars, and a menacing chorus
tracks.bio: British post-grunge. The fly is 'a real fly. And also capitalism.'

tracks.style: Glossy teen dance-pop with romantic verses and a bright key-lift chorus
tracks.bio: Britney Alexandra's bright early single, before her flip-phone-era pop reign.

tracks.style: Nerdy fast-talk folk-pop with warm acoustic strumming and stacked conversational harmonies
tracks.bio: Six nerds from Ontario who tour with a thesaurus.

tracks.style: Laid-back west-coast 90s R&B and rap with a rubbery bass groove and smooth call-and-response
tracks.bio: West-coast R&B. Every song is weather as a metaphor for arguing.

tracks.style: west-coast rap, smooth and menacing, recorded live
tracks.bio: Recorded live at a club called The Frosting. Darker than it sounds.

tracks.style: Dreamy confessional alt-rock with pointed verses and a volcanic chorus
tracks.bio: Wrote it in a parking lot in eleven minutes. Tells you at every show.

tracks.style: Dramatic dream-alt with ringing guitars, ethereal female vocals, and a storm-cloud chorus
tracks.bio: Dream-alt. Singer Saoirse hums the parts she hasn't written yet.

tracks.style: Haunted dream-alt with chiming guitars, intimate verses, and a soaring female-vocal bridge
tracks.bio: The follow-up. The ghost is an ex. Or a real ghost. She won't say.

tracks.style: Snotty three-chord pop-punk at breakneck speed with a shout-along chorus
tracks.bio: Pop-punk trio. All three claim they wrote it; none play it the same twice.

tracks.style: Bright mall pop-punk with palm-muted verses and a gang-vocal chorus
tracks.bio: Mall pop-punk. Sang backup for N2U and will deny it to your face.

tracks.style: Earnest arena-grunge with woolly guitars, mumbled verses, and a widescreen chorus
tracks.bio: Flannel arena-grunge. The singer mumbles on purpose.

tracks.style: Sun-baked jam-band folk-rock with hand percussion, chant vocals, and a wandering groove
tracks.bio: Jam-band hippies. At least four drummers. Member count unknown.

00s.name: The Blender
00s.tagline: ALL the hits, ALL the time, ALL AT ONCE.
00s.voiceDirection: Jamie: bright caffeinated female pop-radio voice. The Toad: dopey male, two-word interjections, record separately.

tracks.style: Bright up-tempo dance-pop road anthem with filtered guitars, breakbeat drums, and a sunrise-sized hook
tracks.bio: Dance-pop one-hit-wonder. Best days of your life, allegedly.

tracks.style: Ethereal trance with featherlight vocals, rising filters, and a euphoric take-me-higher build
tracks.bio: Trance producer who performs behind a wall of fog. No one's seen his face.

tracks.style: Sassy 2000s teen-pop with clipped beats, glossy synths, and a confrontational hook; half of the Text Me Back battle
tracks.bio: Teen-pop royalty. Text her back or she moves on.

tracks.style: Sunny bubblegum pop with bright acoustic strums, handclaps, and an unstoppable chorus; the other half of the battle
tracks.bio: The sunny version. Same demand, more sunshine.

tracks.style: ringtone-beat pop, bubbly female vocal
tracks.bio: Every beat is a phone sound. The ringtone costs more than the song.

tracks.style: Compact early-2000s rap with a tense loop, clipped drums, and a relentless final-word hook
tracks.bio: A fast-talking DMV rapper who announces every return to the booth as the last one.

tracks.style: dark synth/electro-pop, moody
tracks.bio: Moody electro-pop, possibly about two robots falling in love.

tracks.style: 00s emo nostalgia, pop-punk
tracks.bio: Emo, named after a parking lot they've never actually visited.

tracks.style: Mall pop-punk with crunchy guitars, a blue-blue-blue hook, and a sneering lead vocal
tracks.bio: Mall pop-punk. Blue, blue, blue — you know the code.

tracks.style: Pop-punk breakup ballad with chiming verses and a full-volume final chorus
tracks.bio: The weepy one. You were close.

tracks.style: Garage blues-rock stomp with a blunt octave riff, dry drums, and basement-show swagger
tracks.bio: A garage-blues two-piece. The drummer and guitarist may or may not be siblings and refuse to clarify.

tracks.style: Raw garage-blues rock with a bruised refrain, dry drums, and a riff that keeps rebuilding itself
tracks.bio: A garage-blues two-piece. The drummer and guitarist may or may not be siblings and refuse to clarify.

tracks.style: Mellow acoustic surf-pop with brushed percussion, breezy phrasing, and a porch-light chorus
tracks.bio: A barefoot beach troubadour who records everything in a hammock.

tracks.file: Every Time I Close My Eyes.mp3
tracks.song: Every Time I Close My Eyes
tracks.style: Sleek early-2000s pop-rock with clipped funk guitar, falsetto hooks, and a radio-polished chorus
tracks.bio: Relentlessly catchy pop-rock. You'll be humming this against your will.

tracks.style: Anthemic new-wave indie rock with pulsing synths, bright guitar, and a neon-lit chorus
tracks.bio: Vegas glam-indie. The frontman wears one sequined glove and won't say why.

tracks.style: Loud retro garage rock with overdriven guitars, handclaps, and a sneering hook
tracks.bio: Loud garage rock. Every song is about the moon, or a girl, sometimes both.

tracks.style: Angular art-school dance-punk with clipped guitars, disco bass, and a deadpan vocal
tracks.bio: Art-school dance-punk named after a historical event they can't spell.

tracks.style: Upbeat piano pop with bittersweet lyrics, rushing drums, and a bright sing-along chorus
tracks.bio: Piano pop: devastating lyrics over an impossibly happy melody.
